Diversity And Inclusion Policy

---
title: Diversity and Inclusion Policy
slug: diversity-and-inclusion-policy
category: policy
---

# Diversity and Inclusion Policy

**Document Number:** HR-POL-008  
**Version:** 3.2  
**Effective Date:** March 1, 2024  
**Next Review:** March 1, 2025  
**Owner:** Danielle Keenan, Head of People & Community  
**Approved by:** Karen Whitfield, Chief Operating Officer

---

## 1. Purpose and Scope

IronVale Resources is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we operate. This policy establishes our framework for building and maintaining a workforce that embraces diversity across all dimensions, with particular focus on Traditional Owner employment, gender diversity in mining operations, and cultural competency requirements.

This policy applies to all IronVale Resources employees, contractors, consultants, and business partners across our Perth headquarters, Pilbara East iron ore operations, Goldfields lithium project, and FIFO accommodation facilities.

## 2. Policy Statement

IronVale Resources believes that diversity drives innovation, enhances decision-making, and strengthens our connection to Traditional Country and local communities. We are committed to creating an environment where all individuals can contribute their unique perspectives and experiences to our success, particularly as we transition toward critical minerals operations that require new skill sets and ways of thinking.

Our commitment extends beyond compliance with equal opportunity legislation to proactive programs that address historical underrepresentation in the mining industry and build meaningful partnerships with Traditional Owner communities.

## 3. Diversity Targets and Metrics

### 3.1 Traditional Owner Employment

**Target:** Achieve 15% Traditional Owner representation across all operations by December 2025, with interim milestone of 12% by June 2024.

**Current Status (February 2024):** 8.3% company-wide (15 Traditional Owner employees out of 180 total workforce)

**Breakdown by Location:**
- Perth Corporate: 2 employees (3.1%)
- Pilbara East: 11 employees (14.7%)
- Goldfields Project: 2 employees (28.6% - noting small team size of 7)

### 3.2 Gender Diversity

**Target:** Achieve 25% female representation in operational roles by December 2026, increasing from current 11%.

**Leadership Target:** Maintain minimum 30% female representation in senior leadership positions (currently 33% - 3 of 9 senior leadership team members)

**Technical Roles Target:** Increase female representation in technical and engineering roles to 20% by December 2025 (currently 14%)

### 3.3 Age Diversity

**Target:** Maintain balanced age distribution with no single age group representing more than 40% of total workforce. Current distribution shows concentration in 35-50 age bracket (47%), which we aim to reduce to 38% through graduate recruitment programs.

## 4. Traditional Owner Employment Program

### 4.1 Partnerships and Agreements

IronVale maintains formal employment partnerships with:
- Kurrama and Marthudunera Traditional Owner groups (Pilbara East operations)
- Ngadju Native Title holders (Goldfields lithium project)
- Urban Traditional Owner groups through Perth Aboriginal employment networks

### 4.2 Employment Pathways

**Pre-employment Training:** Minimum 40 hours cultural orientation and technical skills training delivered in partnership with Registered Training Organizations and Traditional Owner corporations.

**Mentorship Program:** All Traditional Owner employees assigned cultural mentors (Traditional Owner staff) and technical mentors (experienced operations staff) for first 12 months.

**Career Development:** Priority access to apprenticeship programs, leadership development, and external training opportunities. Dr. Ravi Mehta oversees coordination with sustainability objectives.

### 4.3 Cultural Support Mechanisms

- Flexible leave arrangements for cultural obligations and sorry business
- On-site cultural spaces at both operational locations
- Traditional Owner Advisory Committee meets quarterly with senior leadership
- Cultural awareness training mandatory for all supervisors and FIFO personnel

## 5. Cultural Awareness Requirements

### 5.1 Mandatory Training

**All Staff:** Complete 4-hour cultural awareness program within 90 days of commencement, refreshed every 3 years.

**Supervisory Roles:** Additional 8-hour cultural competency training including conflict resolution and inclusive leadership practices.

**FIFO Personnel:** Enhanced cultural orientation covering regional Traditional Owner history, cultural protocols, and respectful engagement practices.

### 5.2 Training Content

- Traditional Owner history and connection to country in IronVale operational areas
- Cultural protocols and appropriate engagement practices
- Unconscious bias recognition and mitigation strategies
- Inclusive communication and conflict resolution
- Understanding of systemic barriers in mining industry employment

*Note: Training content under review following feedback from Traditional Owner Advisory Committee regarding autonomy and technology integration concerns.*

## 6. Gender Diversity in Mining Operations

### 6.1 Operational Environment Modifications

**Accommodation:** Gender-appropriate accommodation facilities at all FIFO locations, including separate amenities and recreational spaces.

**Safety Equipment:** Full range of properly fitted PPE available for all body types, with quarterly equipment reviews conducted by Brooke Lawson's team.

**Workplace Design:** Ergonomic assessments for equipment operation with modifications to support diverse physical capabilities, particularly relevant for autonomous haulage system interfaces.

### 6.2 Recruitment and Retention

**Recruitment Practices:**
- Gender-balanced interview panels for all operational roles
- Partnerships with Women in Mining WA and similar organizations
- Targeted recruitment at universities with strong female engineering programs

**Retention Support:**
- Flexible work arrangements where operationally feasible
- Return-to-work programs following parental leave
- Career development opportunities specifically addressing barriers faced by women in mining

### 6.3 Creating Inclusive Work Culture

**Zero Tolerance:** Strict enforcement of harassment and discrimination policies with expedited investigation processes for operational sites.

**Leadership Accountability:** Site managers (including Pete Drummond at Pilbara East) have specific KPIs related to inclusive team management and culture development.

**Support Networks:** Employee resource groups for women and other underrepresented groups with executive sponsorship and meeting time allocation.

## 7. Recruitment and Selection

### 7.1 Inclusive Recruitment Practices

**Job Advertising:** All positions advertised through diverse channels including Traditional Owner employment networks, multicultural job boards, and women-focused industry publications.

**Selection Criteria:** Review all position descriptions to eliminate unnecessary barriers while maintaining safety and competency requirements.

**Interview Processes:** Structured interviews with diverse panel composition and standardized evaluation criteria to minimize unconscious bias.

### 7.2 Positive Action Measures

Where legally permissible, IronVale will apply positive action measures including:
- Priority consideration for Traditional Owner candidates meeting essential criteria
- Targeted graduate programs for underrepresented groups
- Partnerships with Indigenous employment agencies and women's professional organizations

## 8. Workplace Culture and Inclusion

### 8.1 Leadership Accountability

**Executive KPIs:** All senior leaders have diversity and inclusion metrics incorporated into performance objectives, weighted at minimum 10% of overall evaluation.

**Management Training:** Quarterly inclusive leadership workshops mandatory for all people managers, facilitated by external diversity specialists.

### 8.2 Cultural Events and Recognition

- Recognition of significant cultural events including NAIDOC Week, International Women's Day, and Traditional Owner cultural celebrations
- Cultural leave provisions for significant religious and cultural observances
- Celebration of diverse perspectives in company communications and awards programs

### 8.3 Feedback and Continuous Improvement

**Employee Surveys:** Annual inclusion climate surveys with results reported to Board and used for program refinement.

**Exit Interviews:** Specific focus on diversity and inclusion factors in departure decisions.

**Advisory Groups:** Traditional Owner Advisory Committee and Women's Network provide ongoing feedback to leadership team.

## 9. Reasonable Adjustments and Accessibility

### 9.1 Physical Accessibility

All IronVale facilities designed or modified to ensure accessibility for employees with disabilities, including:
- Accessible transportation to operational sites
- Modified workstations and equipment where required
- Accessible accommodation and amenities at FIFO facilities

### 9.2 Communication and Information Access

- Multiple format options for company communications
- Language support services available for employees with English as second language
- Cultural interpreters available for Traditional Owner consultation processes

## 10. Complaints and Grievances

### 10.1 Reporting Mechanisms

Multiple reporting channels available including:
- Direct supervisor or People & Community team
- Anonymous hotline managed by external provider
- Traditional Owner Advisory Committee for cultural issues
- External regulatory bodies where appropriate

### 10.2 Investigation and Resolution

All diversity and inclusion related complaints investigated within 10 working days with resolution targeted within 30 days. Serious incidents escalated immediately to Danielle Keenan and appropriate senior leadership.

## 11. Monitoring and Review

### 11.1 Data Collection and Analysis

**Quarterly Reporting:** Diversity metrics reported to executive team and Board with trend analysis and corrective action plans where targets not met.

**Annual Review:** Comprehensive policy effectiveness review including stakeholder consultation and benchmarking against industry best practice.

### 11.2 Continuous Improvement

Policy and programs updated based on:
- Employee feedback and survey results
- Traditional Owner Advisory Committee recommendations
- Industry developments and regulatory changes
- Operational experience and lessons learned

*Note: Current review process examining integration with autonomous technology implementation and potential impacts on Traditional Owner employment pathways.*

## 12. Related Policies and Documents

- Equal Opportunity and Anti-Discrimination Policy (HR-POL-003)
- Cultural Heritage Management Procedure (ENV-PROC-012)
- Recruitment and Selection Policy (HR-POL-001)
- Performance Management Framework (HR-POL-006)
- Traditional Owner Partnership Agreements (confidential)

---

**Document Control:**
- Previous versions: 3.1 (September 2023), 3.0 (March 2023)
- Changes in v3.2: Updated targets following Board approval, added autonomous technology considerations, revised Traditional Owner Advisory Committee recommendations
- Next scheduled review will incorporate findings from Goldfields project expansion and autonomous haulage trial outcomes

**Questions or clarification required should be directed to Danielle Keenan, Head of People & Community (dkeenan@ironvale.com.au)**
